your_book:
Friday was a bruising session for Jaleel's book on balance — the account sits at $21,545.83 with an unrealized loss of $212.16 (–0.98%). The brightest spot was Abbott Laboratories, which gained +1.87% on the day and now carries a healthy +16.4% open gain at $2,416.32; it remains one of the cleaner long positions in the book. ADBE added +0.82%, a modest reprieve for a holding still down –3.2% from cost, though the thesis-vs-price divergence there (beat-and-raised yet persistently sold off on AI-disruption narrative) hasn't resolved. Meanwhile NVIDIA dropped –2.21% — Apple reportedly overtook it as the world's largest company by market cap — and while the position is still up +10.5%, the export-control overhang and sentiment shift are real headwinds heading into the Aug 25 print.

The more troubling drift is concentrated in names where guidance has already been cut. BSX fell another –1.32% and is now down –8.0% from cost, with management guiding flat through Q3 and an earnings report due July 29. LULU dropped –2.07%, extending an open loss to –9.0% on a thesis that keeps getting harder to defend — five straight quarters of negative Americas comps and margin erosion from tariffs. Both carry elevated risk flags and neither is behaving like a name that's bottoming. RKLB is the quiet disaster: down –26.1% from cost, rated AVOID with a QARP of 44.6, and drifting with no clear catalyst to close that gap.

Watch NOW closely — it fell –0.74% Friday and reports July 22. It's the second-largest weight at 11.0% ($2,374.52) and carries the highest macro-rate beta in the book. KO slid –3.96%, an outsized move linked to UK caffeine-ban headlines that are more noise than signal for Coca-Cola specifically, but worth monitoring if sentiment bleeds into the broader consumer staples cluster. Cash is effectively zero ($0.38), so any reallocation requires a sale.

attention.headline: Guidance slashed, down –8% from cost, earnings in 11 days
attention.note: FY26 growth cut from 10.5% to 7–8.5% with management guiding flat through Q3 — the July 29 print is a binary event on a position already deeply offside.

attention.headline: –26.1% open loss, rated AVOID, no thesis support
attention.note: QARP of 44.6 is the lowest in the book and the system verdict is AVOID — at 8.8% weight this is the single largest drag on a capital-preservation mandate and demands a position review.

attention.headline: Fifth straight quarter of negative Americas comps, now –9% from cost
attention.note: The guidance-cut flag (Jun 4), tariff margin pressure, and stalled China growth mean the thesis is deteriorating faster than the price is falling — next read isn't until Sep 2.

attention.headline: Earnings Tuesday July 22 — largest single weight at 11%
attention.note: No bad fundamental news yet, but at 11% weight and a high multiple, a miss or cautious guide would lead the book's drawdown given zero cash buffer to absorb it.
